name: Pawn Max II
description_short: Pawn Max is a Tampa Bay-area pawn shop chain with 9 FL locations offering collateral-based loans and retail merchandise available in-store and online.
description_long:
Pawn Max operates a regional chain of pawn shops anchored in the Tampa Bay area of Florida. The chain runs at least nine numbered locations — Pawn Max 2 through Pawn Max 10 — spread across communities including Oldsmar, Tampa, St. Petersburg, Dunedin, Pinellas Park, Zephyrhills, Hudson, and Port Richey. The Pawn Max 2 location specifically is situated at 3696 Tampa Rd. in Oldsmar, FL 34677, and can be reached at 813.891.1200.

As a pawn operation, Pawn Max's core offering is collateral-based lending: customers bring in personal property, receive a short-term loan secured by that item, and can reclaim the item by repaying the loan plus fees within the agreed term. Items not reclaimed are resold in-store or online. The chain also runs an e-commerce storefront — evident from the site's free-shipping promotion on orders over $50 (excluding freight and shipments to Hawaii and Alaska) — allowing customers to browse and purchase secondhand merchandise remotely.

What distinguishes Pawn Max from a single-location independent pawn shop is its geographic footprint across the greater Tampa Bay region. Nine locations across Hillsborough, Pinellas, and Pasco counties mean customers have multiple options for drop-off, loan pickup, or retail browsing. The branded website and consistent numbering system suggest centralized management rather than a loosely affiliated group, which can mean more standardized processes across branches.

However, the publicly available website content is limited almost entirely to a store locator. No loan terms, interest rates, fee schedules, accepted item categories, or buyback policies are disclosed online. Consumers cannot comparison-shop rates before visiting. Pawn shop loans are inherently high-cost short-term instruments, and without disclosed APR or fee information, borrowers should ask these questions directly in-store before pledging any item.
pros: Nine locations across Hillsborough, Pinellas, and Pasco counties — broad Tampa Bay coverage
Online storefront with merchandise shipping available to continental US (free over $50)
Each location has a direct phone number listed for pre-visit inquiries
Spans diverse communities including Zephyrhills, Hudson, and Port Richey — serves suburban and rural Pasco County
Consistent branding and centralized web presence suggests standardized operations across branches
cons: No loan terms, APR, fee schedules, or accepted item categories disclosed on the website
Free shipping excludes freight items and does not apply to Hawaii or Alaska
Limited online transparency — consumers cannot compare rates or policies before visiting in person
No information about hours of operation published on the locations page
best_for: Tampa Bay-area residents needing quick collateral-based cash without a credit check
Secondhand shoppers looking for electronics, jewelry, or tools available for online purchase
Pasco County residents in Hudson or Port Richey with limited nearby pawn shop options
diagnosis: Pawn Max is best suited for Tampa Bay-area consumers who need fast cash against personal property and want the convenience of a multi-location regional chain rather than a single independent shop. The main caveat is that loan costs, accepted items, and fee structures are not published online, so borrowers must visit or call a branch to understand the actual terms before committing any collateral.
services: Multi-location drop-off and pickup across Tampa Bay
Phone-based location inquiries at each branch
